title: Operation: Knightfall
content:

**Jedi Master [[Minas Velti]]†{{Kenobi|Part I}} **A [[Unidentified Jedi Temple Guard|Jedi Temple Guard]]† *Numerous [[Padawan]]s **Padawan [[Bene]]† **Padawan [[Zett Jukassa]]† **Padawan [[Whie Malreaux]]† *Numerous [[Jedi Initiate|Youngling]]s **Youngling [[Faris]]† **A [[Unidentified Jedi youngling|Jedi youngling]]† **[[Din Grogu|Grogu]]{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 20: The Foundling}} **[[Reva Sevander]]{{Kenobi|Part V}} *1 stolen BARC speeder *5 Naboo Guards *1 [[H-type Nubian yacht]] **''[[Heyblibber Duck]]'' |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Numerous [[Cloning|clone]] [[trooper]]s *Some clone shock troopers *1 LAAT/i |casual2=*Most of the Jedi *At least 2 Naboo Guards |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|First I want you to go to the Jedi Temple. We will catch them off-balance. Do what must be done. Lord Vader. Do not hesitate. Show no mercy.|Darth Sidious, to Darth Vader|audio=ShowNoMercy-RevengeOfTheSith.flac|}} '''Operation: Knightfall''', also known as the '''siege of the Jedi Temple''' and the '''attack on the Jedi Temple''', was one of the first military engagements in the [[Great Jedi Purge]] that took place in the [[Standard year|year]] [[19 BBY]], around the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant]], the home of the [[Jedi Order]]. Following an [[Duel in Palpatine's office|attempt]] of the [[Master of the Order]] [[Mace Windu]] on arresting [[Supreme Chancellor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]], who had been revealed as the [[Sith Lord]] [[Darth]] Sidious at the end of the [[Clone Wars]], [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] turned to the [[dark side of the Force]] and joined the [[Sith]]. Becoming a [[Sith apprentice]] to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]], Skywalker was given the name "Darth Vader" by his [[Sith Master]] and ordered to lead the [[clone trooper]]s of the [[501st Legion]] in a direct assault on the [[Jedi temple|Temple]]. While thousands of [[Jedi]] were [[Great Jedi Purge|purged]] across [[the galaxy]] as a result of [[Order 66]], the new Sith [[Lord]] and his [[legion]] invaded and occupied the Temple. During the assault, [[Jedi Master]] [[Kelleran Beq]] fled the burning Temple with the [[Jedi Initiate]] [[Din Grogu|Grogu]] in the escape from Coruscant. [[Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa|Bail Organa]] of [[Alderaan]] traveled to the Temple but was turned away at [[blaster]] point by [[Clone Commander]] [[Appo]]. Before leaving, he witnessed Appo's [[soldier]]s gun down [[Zett Jukassa]] when the [[Padawan]] attempted to escape. In the aftermath of the attack, which saw the murder of numerous Jedi, including younglings at Vader's hands, the Sith attempted to lure the survivors into a trap via a [[Jedi beacon|beacon]] signaling all Jedi to return to the Temple. However, their plan was thwarted by the efforts of [[Grand Master]] [[Yoda]] and Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who reprogrammed the beacon to warn all Jedi to stay away from Coruscant. With the fall of the Jedi Temple and the near annihilation of the entire Jedi Order, Sidious issued the [[proclamation of the New Order]], dissolving the [[Galactic Republic]] and replacing it with a [[New Order|new regime]]—the [[Galactic Empire]], ruled by himself as [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]]. Coruscant continued to serve as Sidious' capital [[planet]] throughout the [[Imperial Era]], during which he claimed the Jedi Temple as his private residence and renamed it the Imperial Palace. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Every single Jedi, including your friend, Obi-Wan Kenobi, is now an enemy of the Republic.''"
"''I understand, Master.''"
"''We must move quickly. The Jedi are relentless. If they are not all destroyed, it will be civil war without end.|Darth Sidious and Darth Vader|}} [[File:Tragedy.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Sidious conspired to turn Anakin Skywalker to the dark side of the Force.]] Since their first encounter, [[Supreme Chancellor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]]—secretly the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] Darth Sidious—had taken an interest in [[Anakin Skywalker]], a [[Gender|boy]] [[Force-sensitive|strong]] in [[the Force]] and intent on [[Jedi training|learning]] the ways of the [[Jedi]]. He positioned himself as a mentor and [[Parent|father]] figure to the boy in order to influence his decisions, and eventually make him his [[Sith apprentice|apprentice]]. Skywalker, because of this, trusted Palpatine absolutely, confiding in him his greatest secrets, such as his [[Mission to rescue Shmi Skywalker Lars|slaughter]] of [[Tusken Raider]]s on [[Tatooine]]. Near the end of the [[Clone Wars]], Palpatine invited Skywalker, who had become a [[Jedi Knight]], to a performance of ''[[Squid Lake]]'' in the [[Galaxies Opera House]]. There, he told Skywalker about [[Tragedy of Darth Plagueis the Wise|The Tragedy]] of [[Darth Plagueis]] the Wise, telling him that there were [[Sith]] techniques that he could learn to save people-such as his [[Marriage|wife]]-from [[death]]. Later, Palpatine admitted to Skywalker that he knew how to utilize the [[Dark side of the Force|dark side]] of [[the Force]], and Skywalker realized that he was actually the [[Sith Lord]] that the Jedi had been looking for. The Knight was shocked by this revelation, and he fled to the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] to inform [[Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu]], who took a [[squad]] of Masters to apprehend the Sith. [[File:Bowing_before_a_New_Master.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Skywalker submitted to the dark side as Sidious' Sith apprentice and was given the name of Darth Vader.]] In his [[Emperor's office|office]] on [[Coruscant]], Sidious was [[Duel in Palpatine's office|confronted]] by Windu's group, and all but Windu were quickly killed by the Sith. Sidious and Windu continued fighting until Skywalker arrived. Deciding that Amidala's life was more important to him than his commitment to the Jedi, Skywalker cut off Windu's [[Limb|arm]] to save the Sith [[Lord]], and Sidious [[Force push]]ed Windu out of a window to his death. Skywalker then pledged himself to Sidious's teachings in order to save his wife, and was given the Sith name "[[Darth]] Vader." Sidious took this moment to carefully admit that he did not actually know the secret to preserving life, but Vader evidently did not realize what he had said. With that, Sidious declared the [[Jedi Order]] an enemy of the [[Galactic Republic]], assigning Vader the task of attacking the halls of the Jedi Temple and eliminating the growing "menace." While Vader prepared to begin his mission, Sidious enacted [[Order 66]], a mandate that Republic [[clone trooper]]s across [[the galaxy]] had been programmed to follow without question since before the Clone Wars had broken out. It commanded them to kill their [[Jedi General]]s and [[Jedi Commander]]s in the name of the Republic. Within [[Standard minute|minutes]], many Jedi were killed by their formerly loyal [[troop]]s, though a few survived the Order. For example, Skywalker's close friend and former mentor, [[Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], was targeted by his own troops amid the [[Battle of Utapau]] yet managed to escape. Skywalker's former [[Padawan]] [[Ahsoka Tano]], though no longer a Jedi herself, was [[Skirmish aboard the Tribunal|targeted]] by the [[332nd Division]] and thus unable to try and find a way to help her friend after feeling Skywalker's anguish.{{TCW|Shattered}} On Coruscant, a [[battalion]]''[[Skywalker: A Family at War]]'' of [[trooper]]s from Vader's own [[501st Legion]] under [[Clone Commander]] [[Appo]]{{HelmetCollectionCite|16|Helmets}} marched under his command, giving the new Sith Lord hundreds to thousands of troops with whom he could attack the Jedi Temple. ==The operation== ===Pressing in=== {{Quote|It was like something out of a nightmare. There was this… shadow leading the clones—men I had thought of as my brothers—cutting down anyone who crossed its path. It killed everyone. Anyone who dared face it. Even— Even the younglings.|[[Ryza Ayre]]|{{InsiderCite|233|Galactic Tales: The Failing of the Light}}}} [[File:Knightfall1.png|thumb|left|250px|Vader's first act as a Sith Lord was to lead the 501st Legion in an attack on the Jedi Temple of Coruscant.]] Operation: Knightfall{{EncyclopediaCite|Republic and Separatist Ships (2)}} began when Darth Vader led the 501st, who was Vader's personal legion. Among the ranks included an elite group of [[clone commando]]s. Skywalker brought the legion to the gates of the [[Jedi temple|Jedi Temple]] and brought them inside, where Vader and the clones attacked its residents. The clones were not made aware of Skywalker's new Sith identity and were led to believe he had simply refused to side with the Jedi Order and their treasonous actions. As the Sith Lord marched upon the Temple steps, a [[Unidentified Jedi Temple Guard|Jedi temple guard]] recognized him as his former identity Anakin Skywalker and said "Skywalker" under his breath before being cut down by Vader. The Jedi refused to die without a fight in defense of their home and fought back, killing many clone troopers. The clones, nonetheless, performed well and executed their role in the operation, with Vader taking on those Jedi the clones did not handle[[Darth Vader (2017) 14|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 14]] and cutting down all those he came across. The fierce fighting sparked a blaze which soon engulfed the entire Temple. Padawan [[Ryza Ayre]] bore witness to the massacre and felt betrayed by the clones' actions. Further, Ayre saw Vader's role as their leader but did not have enough time to discern the figure's identity. [[File:Unyielding_EaW.png|thumb|right|200px|Driven by the dark side, the fallen Jedi Knight killed younglings during the Temple attack.]] As fighting raged across the building, Vader went to search the [[Jedi Council Chamber]] for more survivors, and it was there that he found a large group of [[Jedi Initiate|younglings]]. [[Unidentified Jedi youngling|A young boy]] approached Vader from where the other [[youngling]]s had hidden themselves. The boy, not realizing that Skywalker had become Vader and defected to the Sith, asked him what the Jedi were going to do, and in answer, Vader re-ignited [[Skywalker lightsaber|his]] [[lightsaber]] and proceeded to execute the entire group. ===The Organa interlude=== Witnessing the smoke and flames grow from the temple and realizing it was under siege from thousands of clones, [[Bail Prestor Organa|Bail Organa]], the [[senator]] from [[Alderaan]] and a Jedi sympathizer, landed on the landing platform of one of the Tower hangars to investigate the [[fire]]. After being turned away by the clones under Appo guarding the [[hangar]], the retreating Senator witnessed [[Zett Jukassa]], a young [[Padawan]], cutting his way through the group in an attempt to escape the massacre. Although Jukassa succeeded in killing several clones, and injuring a surprised Appo, he was soon overwhelmed and shot dead in front of the horrified senator by [[Clone Lieutenant]] [[Bow (clone trooper)|Bow]]. As Organa tried to escape, the clones opened fire on him as well, but [[Clone Sergeant]] [[Fox (501st Legion)|Fox]] ordered them to stand down, letting the Senator escape. ===Even the younglings=== {{Quote|Palpatine's clones did their part, but the rest… He killed. Even the younglings. The ''younglings''. By the light.|Ferren Barr, on Operation: Knightfall|}} [[File:DiamondJediCrest-BOBF6.png|thumb|left|350px|A group of clone troopers pick off Jedi while approaching Grogu's position.]] At one point during the siege, the [[Force-sensitive]] [[Din Grogu|Grogu]] saw four Jedi killed while trying to protect him. The Jedi sent Grogu to an [[Turbolift|elevator]] to meet [[Jedi Master]]{{InsiderCite|222|Star Wars: The Mandalorian Season Three Companion|''Star Wars: The Mandalorian'' Season Three Companion}} [[Kelleran Beq]] before the clone troopers could reach him. Beq and Grogu were ambushed by clone troopers to which Beq deflected the blaster fire and they soon fled from the temple on a [[BARC speeder]]. In their escape from Coruscant, Beq and Grogu sped through the skies of the city to reach a waiting [[H-type Nubian yacht]] while chased by [[Low Altitude Assault Transport]]s. After a small grouping of [[Naboo Palace Guard|Naboo guards]] held off pursuing clones, both managed to escape the planet aboard the yacht. In other parts of the temple, a group of younglings were busy in a training session under Jedi Master [[Minas Velti]], only for several clones to storm the room and try to kill all present. Velti cut down the clones and ushered her younglings out, where they encountered more clones battling Jedi. On one of the temple's internal bridges, Velti was fatally shot in the stomach but managed to cut down all nearby clones before falling, giving the younglings, led by [[Reva Sevander]], a chance to flee.{{Kenobi|Part I}} Before they could escape the Temple, however, they ran into Vader and a group of clone troopers. The younglings, like their fellows in the Council chamber, thought he was there to help but Vader proceeded to murder the entire class of students, leaving only a wounded Reva alive to hide among the bodies of her friends.{{Kenobi|Part V}} ===Closing actions=== [[File:Jedi_Purge.png|thumb|right|350px|The Jedi continued to fight back against their former allies, but were overwhelmed by the legion's soldiers.]] Elsewhere in the Temple, Vader personally murdered [[Shaak Ti]] by stabbing her through the back during her [[meditation]]. At some stage of the operation, Vader also confronted [[Cin Drallig]], the Temple's battlemaster and one of the most powerful Masters in the Temple. After murdering two of Drallig's trainees, [[Bene]] and [[Whie Malreaux]], the Sith entered a [[Lightsaber duel|duel]] with Drallig; the veteran Jedi fought hard, but ultimately fell to Vader's blade. Vader altered and broadcast a [[Jedi beacon|beacon]] ordering all Jedi to return to the Jedi Temple because the war was over, leading any who heeded the call to their death at the hands of the clones.{{SW|url=news/hidden-knowledge-5-fascinating-facts-about-the-jedi-temple|text=Hidden Knowledge: 5 Fascinating Facts about the Jedi Temple|nolive=1}} As the fighting began to die down, Darth Sidious arrived at the Temple to check on Vader's progress, wishing to see if Vader had indeed wiped out all Jedi as commanded.[[Darth Vader (2020) 7|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 7]] Vader knelt before his master to be congratulated by the Dark Lord, who was pleased by his new apprentice's actions and ordered him to bring peace to the [[Galactic Empire]]; Sidious then ordered Vader to travel to [[Mustafar]] to [[Mission to Mustafar|eliminate]] the [[Executive Separatist Council|leaders]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]], assembled there by Sidious under the pretense of a safe haven. ==Aftermath== ===The Sith triumphant=== {{Quote|You have done well, my new apprentice. Now, Lord Vader, go and bring peace to the Empire.|Darth Sidious, to Darth Vader|}} [[File:FlamingTemple.png|thumb|left|250px|The fire that burned the Jedi Temple could be seen from Padmé Amidala's apartment.]] In the aftermath of the Temple attack, Vader returned to his wife at [[Padmé's Apartment|her apartment]]. At the time, Amidala was unaware that her husband had adopted a new identity and therefore believed that he was still Anakin Skywalker. Amidala's confusion about the attack on the Jedi Temple was replaced by disbelief when Vader accused the Jedi of committing treason and rebellion. Additionally, Vader revealed that he had witnessed Mace Windu's attempt to assassinate the Supreme Chancellor. He disavowed his ties to the Jedi Order and declared his allegiance to Chancellor Palpatine, the [[Galactic Senate]], and Amidala. Vader left his wife on Coruscant to execute the next mission for his master. Before leaving, he told Amidala that his destination was Mustafar, where the Separatist leaders were hiding, and that his intention was to bring a definitive end to the Clone Wars. Vader implored Amidala to remain in the capital and await his return. [[File:Declaration.png|thumb|right|250px|With the Jedi exterminated and the Republic under his control, Sidious declared himself Emperor of the Galactic Empire.]] Before the fires had been doused, a [[special session of Congress]] was called by Palpatine the [[Standard day|day]] after the attack on the Temple. During the assembly, he informed the senators of the Jedi's attempt to usurp control of the galaxy and the previous night's events. He also told them of the Temple's pacification and the clones' efforts across the galaxy to wipe out the Jedi Order in the wake of that realization. To fully sway his audience to his cause, Palpatine delivered a [[Proclamation of the New Order|speech]] claiming the Jedi had orchestrated a rebellion. He also announced that to establish order and security throughout the galaxy, the Galactic Republic would be reorganized as a unified body under his sole, absolute authority, the [[Galactic Empire]]. ===Return to the Temple=== {{Quote|Not even the Younglings survived.''"
"''Killed not by clones, this Padawan, by a lightsaber, he was.''"
"''Who? Who could have done this?|Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda|}} {{Main|Infiltration of the Jedi Temple}} [[File:Pg3_saber_bg.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Jedi Masters Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi returned to Coruscant where they killed some of the clones who purged the Temple.]] Following the raid, Masters [[Yoda]]—who had managed to escape Order 66 amid the [[Battle of Kashyyyk]] and was sought out by Senator Organa immediately following his escape—and Kenobi received the message ordering all Jedi to return to the Temple and immediately recognized it as a trap. Since the emergency Senate session would make it easier to enter the Temple, the two Jedi Masters [[Infiltration of the Jedi Temple|returned to the Temple]] during the meeting to take advantage of it. They found the Temple in shambles, fires still burning, and bodies from both sides of the conflict strewn through the halls. Coming across a group of murdered younglings, Yoda examined the body of one Padawan and concluded that he had been slain by someone wielding a lightsaber, a notion that Kenobi could not fathom. After fighting his way through their former allies alongside Yoda, Kenobi was able to enter the beacon room. There, Kenobi reconfigured the beacon to warn Jedi away instead of calling them to the temple, which was an idea inspired by a question from the youngling [[Kanan Jarrus|Caleb Dume]] earlier in the Clone Wars.[[A New Dawn (novel)|''A New Dawn'']] Dume himself had [[Battle of Kaller|survived the initial purge]] thanks to the sacrifice of his master, [[Depa Billaba]],{{TBB|Aftermath (episode)}} and been on his way to Coruscant due to the false message, but received Kenobi's warning through his [[Kanan Jarrus' holocron|holocron]] as he was spotted by Imperial [[Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter|ARC-170 starfighters]]. Dume was able to [[Ambush in the Coruscant system|escape]] the Imperial ships,[[Kanan 2|''Kanan'' 2]] and he ultimately went on to change his name to "Kanan Jarrus." Jarrus eventually became an important member of the early [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]], and even trained his own Jedi Padawan, [[Ezra Bridger]].{{Rebels|DUME}} [[File:Anakin_Obi-Wan_Clash.png|thumb|right|250px|Having learned of Skywalker's descent to the dark side, Kenobi confronted his former pupil on the fiery world of Mustafar.]] After successfully altering the beacon, Kenobi accessed the security [[hologram]] in an attempt to discover who the lightsaber wielder that led the massacre was, witnessing it recording Vader killing Cin Drallig and his Padawans before conferring with Sidious. Kenobi was shocked by the revelation that Skywalker, his former Padawan, had fallen to the dark side and massacred his fellow Jedi. In order to find him, he went to Amidala, who had been watching the Jedi Temple burn from afar, and stowed away in secret on her [[Starship|ship]] when she went to confront her husband on Mustafar. This led to a [[Duel on Mustafar|duel]] between the two Force users. Before her murder at Darth Vader's hand, Shaak Ti managed to record a message on a [[holocron]] urging any future readers to carry on the Order and not let the Jedi die in vain. This holocron would end up in the collection of [[Grakkus Jahibakti Tingi|Grakkus]], a [[Hutt]] [[crime lord]], and the message witnessed by Anakin's son, [[Luke Skywalker]].[[Star Wars (2015) 9|''Star Wars'' (2015) 9]] The youngling Reva managed to survive her wounds and escape the Temple. Filled with rage and grief and desiring revenge for her fallen friends, she fell to the dark side and joined the [[Inquisitorius]], serving under the rank of Third Sister with the intention of getting close to Vader and killing him for his part in the massacre. She would also hold Kenobi responsible for the terrible events of that night, failing to prevent his apprentice from falling into darkness and murdering her friends. ==Behind the scenes== ===Anakin's fall=== {{Quote|Then [Sidious] says 'Okay, well, now I'll [save your wife], but now you have to do all these things: you have to kill the Jedi because if you don't kill all the Jedi—even if you leave one alive, even if it's just one of the little kids—they'll come back and get us and we'll be dealing with this forever.'|George Lucas in the ''Revenge of the Sith'' commentary|}} [[File:Clone_in_Jedi_disguise.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Cut promotional image of a clone trooper disguised as a Jedi]] The eradication of the Jedi Order and the turning of Anakin Skywalker were first indirectly mentioned in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[original trilogy]]. However, the [[2005]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] depicted the event for the first time. [[George Lucas]], the creator of ''Star Wars'', felt that the portion of the raid featuring the Jedi youngling and his clan was a very necessary one. He wanted to demonstrate just how far Skywalker had fallen from his pedestal as a Jedi Knight, but he had absolutely no intention for actually showing them killed. Nonetheless, another child, Zett Jukassa, was killed on screen. He was portrayed by Lucas's son [[Jett Lucas]].{{Film|III}} DVD commentary In the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series episode "[[Ghosts of Mortis]]," the [[Son]] shows Anakin Skywalker a vision of his future that included portions of the events of ''Revenge of the Sith''. One of these visions was of Skywalker raising his lightsaber to strike down a child immediately after a vision of Palpatine using Force lightning, and before he saw his strangling Padmé Amidala with the Force just before his duel with Obi-Wan Kenobi. Taken as chronological visions of the movie, and interpreting Palpatine's scene as from the showdown with Mace Windu, that would place this child as a victim of the raid. ===Cut content=== Originally, Kenobi and Yoda were to be greeted by a squad of clone troopers disguised as Jedi in the movie. A promotional image of [[Temuera Morrison]] as one of these clones is on the second disc of the ''Revenge of the Sith'' DVD set, suggesting that they were considered for the movie. This scene can be viewed on the 2014 blu-ray release. ===Contradictions=== [[File:Vader-walks-in.png|thumb|right|300px|Darth Vader leads a squad of clone troopers towards Reva's youngling class.]] The [[2016]] sourcebook ''[[Nexus of Power]]'' states that, while the 501st Legion laid siege to the Jedi Temple, Vader led clone commandos into the building to wipe out the Jedi, suggesting 501st troopers only besieged the building, while the internal fighting was carried out by Vader and commandos. However, this is contradicted by ''Revenge of the Sith'', which depicts standard clone troopers of the 501st entering and fighting Jedi within the Temple. This was further affirmed by the [[Disney+]] series ''[[Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett]]'',{{BOBF|Chapter 6: From the Desert Comes a Stranger}} ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: The Mandalorian]]'', which all depicted the internal battle between the 501st and the Jedi. ''Revenge of the Sith'' and ''Kenobi'' also depict Vader leading regular clone troopers into the internal battle. As such, ''Nexus of Power'' must be incorrect: the 501st both besieged the building and attacked the inside of the Jedi Temple. Regular 501st soldiers therefore fought alongside commandos deployed into the engagement. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox| *''[[Star Wars: The Prequel Trilogy – A Graphic Novel]]'' *{{InsiderCite|233|The Failing of the Light}} *''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]'' {{Ab|Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *{{TCW|Ghosts of Mortis}} {{Vision|Anakin Skywalker}} *{{TCW|Voices}} {{Vision|Yoda}}
